mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which lifts equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon when both series rise concurrently.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 0.5% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rising industrial production signals capacity utilization normalization, which expands profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as supply constraints ease.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns turn negative when INDPRO growth exceeds 1.5% month-over-month, indicating supply glut fears override margin expansion benefits.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent annually signals robust aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal predicts positive returns in fewer than 40% of observed quarters, or the forward return distribution shows no median difference between above-2% and below-2% GDP growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers inflation hedging flows into equities, compressing real yields and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of months where CPI exceeds the 0.4% threshold, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in the unemployment rate and increases in total nonfarm payrolls confirm labor market healing, which reduces equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive information ratio over a 3-year walk-forward test, or the interaction term becomes negative when UNRATE drops below 7.0%.

mechanism_narrative: In early-cycle expansion phases, rising retail sales combined with stable inflation signal sustainable demand growth without price pressures, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL rises above 0.3% month-over-month despite RSAFS growth, indicating stagflation fears override demand signals.
